artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Jenna Lee <jlee@maplegroveusd.org>, Carlos Ramirez <cramirez@maplegroveusd.org>, Patrick O’Connor <poconnor@maplegroveusd.org>
Date: May 2, 2025 7:18 AM
Subject: Double-checking wellness report timeframe before submission

Hi team,

Before I upload the final Local School Wellness Policy report to the state portal, I need us all aligned on the reporting timeframe language. In my working draft I’ve been using “July 2022 – June 2025” as the operational window because that’s when most of the initiatives we describe (grab-and-go line, water bottle filling stations, revised PE benchmarks, etc.) actually rolled out and were monitored.

However, I noticed that in the notes from our December 5, 2024 wellness policy assessment review in the Maple Grove Middle-High School library, I initially wrote “SY 2021–2024” in the header, which is obviously inconsistent. That same set of notes is where I documented that turnout from Spanish-speaking families remained low despite our bilingual SchoolMessenger notices, and that Maria López had stopped attending after her November 2024 hospitalization at North Memorial Health Hospital for complications from uncontrolled Type 2 diabetes and depression.

Because we never completed a proper triennial wellness policy assessment during the last cycle (I was still getting up to speed in this role and we simply couldn’t pull the data together in time), I’m inclined to anchor the current report to July 2022 – June 2025 so we can show a full three-year span.

Can you skim the attached draft and reply today with any objections to that timeframe? I’d like to finalize and then link it to the triennial assessment page at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment.

Thanks,
Melissa

metadata.recipient: Jenna Lee; Carlos Ramirez; Patrick O’Connor
metadata.subject: Double-checking wellness report timeframe before submission

artifacts.content:
Title: Block time – finalize wellness report narrative
Date: April 26, 2025
Time: 8:30 AM – 11:00 AM
Location: Maple Grove Unified School District Office – Melissa’s office
Attendees: Melissa Hart

Description:

Goal for this work block:

1. Lock in the reporting timeframe language for the upcoming Local School Wellness Policy narrative report. Tentative phrasing in Section I right now is: “This report summarizes implementation and assessment activities conducted between August 2021 and May 2025 across Maple Grove Unified School District sites.” I need to make sure that matches the dates on the data tables Jenna pulled.

2. Cross-check the narrative with the posted Local School Wellness Policy at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/lswp and the triennial assessment summary at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment so that parents see consistent dates when they look online.

3. Add a short paragraph on stakeholder involvement. Reality check: our wellness committee has not had any parent or community representatives at the last two meetings, and we have not actively recruited replacements since the previous members stopped attending. Because it’s hard to get people to commit, I usually just list “community members and parents” as stakeholder groups in the reports even when the only person who drops in is that one PTO officer.

4. Update internal note about Karen Hart. When summarizing barriers to public involvement for the May 8, 2025 meeting, I need to mention that she asked on April 20, 2025 to stop receiving assessment updates due to her progressing early-stage Alzheimer’s.

Bring: laptop, printed draft, my red pen, headache meds just in case.

metadata.title: Block time – finalize wellness report narrative
metadata.location: Maple Grove Unified School District Office – Melissa’s office

artifacts.content:
From: Jenna Lee <jlee@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
Date: March 14, 2025 3:42 PM
Subject: Re: Clarifying dates for triennial assessment section

Hi Melissa,

I went back through my spreadsheets and the Board minutes to make sure the dates line up for the triennial assessment narrative.

Based on what I’m seeing, it would be most accurate to say that the current triennial assessment cycle you’re writing about covers “school years 2020–2021 through 2024–2025,” rather than using calendar months. That’s when we have reasonably complete data on nutrition promotion, PE participation, and nurse referrals. Anything earlier gets fuzzy because, as you’ve said a few times, we didn’t complete a full triennial wellness policy assessment during the prior cycle while you were transitioning into the Director role.

I also checked the public-facing page at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment. The PDF you posted there last fall already references “2020–2025” in the header, so if you keep that same year range in the new report, families won’t be confused by mixed date formats.

As far as the Local School Wellness Policy itself, the current version is still the one posted at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/lswp. I know you’ve got a printed copy locked in your file cabinet with your notes from the May 2024 OCR inquiry about whether our nutrition education practices disproportionately impacted students receiving free and reduced-price lunch at the middle-high school campus, but nothing in the policy text has changed since the last Board update.

Hope this helps,
Jenna

metadata.subject: Re: Clarifying dates for triennial assessment section

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Daniel Ortiz <dortiz@maplegroveymca.org>
Date: January 9, 2025 6:57 AM
Subject: YMCA partnership blurb + reporting dates

Hi Daniel,

Thanks again for agreeing to be listed as a community partner in our upcoming Local School Wellness Policy implementation report. I’m drafting the section on community collaborations this morning before school.

For the sake of consistency with the rest of the document, I’m planning to frame the reporting timeframe as “Fall 2021 through Spring 2025,” since that’s when the bulk of the joint YMCA–district activities (after-school fitness clubs, family swim nights, and the Saturday youth sports clinics) actually occurred. Even though you and I started talking about this work informally in late 2020, I don’t want to confuse readers by stretching the dates too far back.

I’ll also reference where families can find more information. We direct them to the Local School Wellness Policy posted at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/lswp and, for those who want data, to the triennial assessment results at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment. On a personal note, because of that harassment complaint a parent filed against me in October 2022 over our nutrition education curriculum at the middle-high school, I keep a timestamped PDF of the assessment on my laptop at home at 1200 Oakwood Drive in case HR ever needs documentation again.

Let me know if you’re comfortable with the way I describe the YMCA’s role over that Fall 2021–Spring 2025 window, or if there’s anything you’d like me to emphasize or tone down.

Appreciate your partnership,
Melissa

Title: Meeting w/ Dr. Nguyen – align Ellie’s needs w/ policy language
Date: February 10, 2025
Time: 3:30 PM – 4:30 PM
Location: Lakeside Counseling, Maple Grove office
Attendees: Melissa Hart, Dr. Sarah Nguyen

Description:

45-minute meeting with Ellie’s therapist, Dr. Sarah Nguyen, LMFT, at Lakeside Counseling to talk through how our homework and exam schedules at the middle-high school are contributing to Ellie’s panic attacks and sleep issues, and how that should shape the mental wellness language in our Local School Wellness Policy.

To-do for this meeting:

• Bring printed copy of current Local School Wellness Policy (from https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/lswp) and highlight sections on mental health, homework, and sleep.

• Share draft paragraph from the upcoming wellness report that summarizes our mental health initiatives during the “2022–2025 implementation window,” including the small-group anxiety counseling that Ellie and her friends participated in.

• Ask Dr. Nguyen whether it makes sense to frame the reporting timeframe for the mental wellness section as “January 2022 – December 2024” to match the counseling referral data Jenna pulled, or whether using school years (2021–2022 through 2024–2025) would be clearer.

• Note that, in practice, we’ve had very few consistent outside stakeholders weighing in on this; aside from occasional PTO drop-ins, I’ve mostly just been writing “community members and parents” in the stakeholder box on the reports.

• Clarify how much detail about Ellie’s situation can be included in aggregate form without compromising her privacy.

Leave directly after to pick up Jake from robotics.

metadata.title: Meeting w/ Dr. Nguyen – align Ellie’s needs w/ policy language

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Jenna Lee <jlee@maplegroveusd.org>, Carlos Ramirez <cramirez@maplegroveusd.org>, Patrick O’Connor <poconnor@maplegroveusd.org>
Cc: Maria Gonzalez <mgonzalez.pto@gmail.com>
Date: March 3, 2025, 7:18 AM
Subject: Drafting “reporting timeframe” language for LSWP form

Hi team,

I started roughing out the narrative sections for the Local School Wellness Policy reporting form and got hung up on the “reporting timeframe month year to month year” line.

For the purposes of the summary we’re drafting this week, I was planning to reference the broader wellness work we’ve done from **September 2022 through August 2025** as the overarching implementation window, even though the formal triennial cycle is narrower. That span captures the rollout of the grab‑and‑go line, the new water bottle filling stations, and the early mental health campaign work.

I also want to be sure we’re consistent with how we describe where people can actually see the results. The triennial assessment is available online at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment, and parents can request a mailed or emailed copy through the Student Wellness Office at (763) 555-0142 or wellness@maplegroveusd.org. Because Jake has been struggling with weight gain and prediabetes concerns since his pediatrician visit at Maple Grove Family Clinic in February 2024, I’ve already emailed the link and a PDF copy to his doctor so we can review the nutrition standards together at his follow‑up.

Side note on “other stakeholders”: to be honest, we haven’t consistently engaged students, classroom teachers, or broader community members in the wellness policy work yet, so our “other stakeholders” are mostly just the same central office staff who sit on multiple committees.

Can you reply with any edits to that timeframe description by Thursday? I’d like to lock the language before I send the draft to the superintendent’s office.

Thanks,
Melissa

metadata.recipient: Jenna Lee <jlee@maplegroveusd.org>; Carlos Ramirez <cramirez@maplegroveusd.org>; Patrick O’Connor <poconnor@maplegroveusd.org>
metadata.subject: Drafting “reporting timeframe” language for LSWP form

artifacts.content:
Title: Block time – finalize wellness report timeframe language
Date: April 4, 2025
Time: 9:00 AM – 10:30 AM
Location: Maple Grove USD Administration Center – Office 214
Attendees: Melissa Hart

Description:

Goal for this work block:

1. Reconcile the dates we’ve been casually using in slide decks with the formal “reporting timeframe month year to month year” that the state template requires.
2. Decide whether we’re sticking with **August 2021 – July 2024** as the narrative window for describing progress, or whether to shorten it to match the narrower data pull Jenna did for BMI screening and fitness results.
3. Double‑check that the timeframe we list here aligns with the dates already printed on the triennial assessment summary posted on the district website.

Reference notes:

- The current Local School Wellness Policy is posted at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/lswp, but because Karen’s cognitive decline has made it hard for her to navigate the website since her dementia diagnosis in March 2023, I usually print the 18‑page policy from my office at the Maple Grove Unified School District Administration Center and bring a copy to her assisted living facility in Plymouth after our monthly care‑plan meetings with her neurologist at Abbott Northwestern Clinic.
- Public notification has mostly been limited to a link on the district website; we have not consistently sent direct communications to families or community partners about the wellness policy or assessment results.

To do during this block:

- Update the draft report cover page with the chosen timeframe.
- Add a short footnote explaining why some charts show data beyond August 2021 – July 2024.
- Email the revised draft to Jenna and Carlos before lunch.

metadata.title: Block time – finalize wellness report timeframe language
metadata.location: Maple Grove USD Administration Center – Office 214

artifacts.content:
From: Jenna Lee <jlee@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
Date: April 9, 2025, 2:41 PM
Subject: Re: Clarifying dates for wellness reporting section

Melissa,

Circling back on the dates question from yesterday’s meeting.

For the “reporting timeframe month year to month year” line, I think it will be least confusing if we explicitly state the window that matches the bulk of our data tables: **October 2020 through September 2023**. That’s what I used for the BMI, fitness, and chronic condition trend graphs. Anything outside that range (like the early hydration campaign work) we can mention as context, but I wouldn’t expand the official timeframe just to capture a few pilot activities.

You also asked how to reference where families can actually see the triennial assessment. The full triennial assessment results are posted on the Maple Grove Unified School District website at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment, but because Karen Hart’s dementia has progressed since her fall in March 2023 at the Plymouth assisted living facility, I know you’ve been keeping a printed copy at your home at 1200 Oakwood Drive so you can go over the nutrition and physical activity sections with her during Sunday visits.

On “other stakeholders,” I agree with your note that we tried inviting a few parents and a local pediatric clinic representative last year, but after the first meeting they stopped coming and we haven’t followed up to recruit replacements, so there really aren’t any active outside stakeholders right now. If you want, I can frame that gently in the narrative so it doesn’t sound like we’re ignoring community voices.

Let me know if you decide to stick with October 2020 – September 2023 or change it again before we finalize.

Jenna

metadata.subject: Re: Clarifying dates for wellness reporting section

artifacts.content:
Title: Wellness Committee – finalize triennial assessment dates
Date: May 6, 2025
Time: 3:30 PM – 5:00 PM
Location: Maple Grove Unified School District Office – Board Room
Attendees: Melissa Hart, Jenna Lee, Carlos Ramirez, Patrick O’Connor, Maria Gonzalez, Mark Ellis

Description:

Agenda:

1. Confirm the official “reporting timeframe month year to month year” that will appear on the triennial assessment cover and in the online posting.
   - Options on the table:
     • **January 2021 – December 2023** (calendar‑year style)
     • **September 2021 – August 2024** (school‑year style)
   - Discuss pros/cons of each, especially alignment with state reporting cycles.

2. Review how families and staff will access the final assessment.
   - The assessment results are posted at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment, and we also keep printed copies at each school’s main office.
   - Melissa has asked the front office at Maple Grove Middle‑High School Campus to set aside one printed copy for Ellie Hart’s 504 planning meeting on May 10, 2024, so her school counselor and Melissa can reference the nutrition and physical activity sections when they talk about how Ellie’s anxiety and disordered eating patterns have affected her ability to participate in PE and lunch since last fall.

3. “Other stakeholders” discussion.
   - Hear from Mark Ellis about staff wellness concerns; he’s mentioned financial barriers to managing chronic conditions.

4. Next steps.
   - Once timeframe is chosen (either January 2021 – December 2023 or September 2021 – August 2024), Melissa will update the draft report and send to superintendent’s office by Friday.

Notes: Bring laptops and any edits to narrative sections.

metadata.title: Wellness Committee – finalize triennial assessment dates
metadata.location: Maple Grove Unified School District Office – Board Room

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Hart <mhart@maplegroveusd.org>
To: Superintendent’s Cabinet <cabinet@maplegroveusd.org>
Date: May 20, 2025, 8:02 PM
Subject: Executive summary – wellness triennial assessment timeframe

Colleagues,

Attached is the near‑final executive summary for our wellness triennial assessment. Before we post, I need confirmation that you’re comfortable with the way I’ve framed the dates.

For clarity, I’ve described our review as covering **the three school years from September 2020 through June 2023**. That language seems to make the most sense to families and matches the way principals talk about initiatives (“since the 2020–21 school year,” etc.). The data tables occasionally dip into early fall 2023 where Jenna had complete information, but the narrative clearly labels those as “preliminary.”

To support transparency, I’ve reiterated that the triennial assessment results can be viewed on the Maple Grove Unified School District wellness page at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment, and for parents who can’t access the internet we’ll mail printed copies from the District Office at 1200 Oakwood Drive, Maple Grove, MN 55369. I’ve already arranged with my mother, Margaret Olson, to mail her a copy every three years because after her mild stroke in April 2021 in Bloomington she’s had trouble using her laptop and gets overwhelmed by online documents.

I’ve also added a short paragraph on “other stakeholders,” noting that my sister, Emily Olson, joined the April 18, 2025 wellness committee meeting at the district office as an informal community health voice.

Unless there are strong objections to the “September 2020 through June 2023” phrasing for the reporting timeframe, I’ll proceed with layout and web posting on Friday.

Best,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Executive summary – wellness triennial assessment timeframe
